title: Angular 18
description: A staff-level deep dive into Angular 18 signals, the new control flow, deferrable views, change detection internals, and the architectural decisions that separate a senior Angular engineer from the rest.

title: ArgoCD & GitOps
description: Master GitOps with ArgoCD: declarative continuous delivery where Git is the single source of truth and a controller continuously reconciles your cluster toward the desired state.

title: AWS EC2
description: A staff-level deep dive into Amazon EC2 — instance families, storage, networking, scaling, and cost models — with the trade-offs you must defend in a senior systems-design interview.

title: AWS ECS
description: A deep, production-grade tour of Amazon ECS: task definitions, services, launch types, awsvpc networking, IAM roles, secrets, autoscaling, and ALB integration — from first principles to incident war stories.

title: AWS EKS
description: A staff-level deep dive into running production Kubernetes on AWS EKS: control plane, node compute models, IRSA, networking, autoscaling, and the operational footguns that page you at 3am.

title: AWS IAM
description: Master AWS Identity and Access Management — from users, roles, and policy evaluation logic to STS assume-role, permission boundaries, SCPs, and IRSA for production-grade, least-privilege security.

title: AWS Lambda
description: A staff-level deep dive into AWS Lambda's execution model, cold starts, concurrency, event sources, and the idempotency patterns that keep at-least-once delivery from corrupting your data.

title: AWS S3
description: Master Amazon S3 from buckets and storage classes to secure direct-to-S3 uploads, lifecycle policies, and the encryption and access-control footguns that cause real-world data leaks.

title: Behavioral Interviews (STAR)
description: Master the STAR method to turn raw engineering experience into crisp, impact-driven stories that signal seniority in behavioral interviews.

title: Caching & Redis
description: A staff-level deep dive into caching strategies and Redis internals: cache patterns, eviction, data structures, distributed locks, and the hard problems of invalidation and cache stampede.

title: CAP Theorem & Distributed Systems
description: A staff-level treatment of CAP and its consequences: what the theorem actually proves, why partition tolerance is mandatory, the consistency-model spectrum, quorum math, replication and consensus (Raft/Paxos), and how to classify and design real systems under partition and latency constraints.

title: CI/CD
description: A staff-level tour of continuous integration and delivery: pipeline design, deployment strategies, artifact promotion, secrets, and the metrics that prove your delivery actually works.

title: Clean Architecture
description: A disciplined approach to structuring software so that business rules stay independent of frameworks, databases, and delivery mechanisms by enforcing a strict inward-pointing dependency rule.

title: Data Structures & Algorithms
description: A staff-level walkthrough of the data structures and algorithmic patterns that decide whether your system scales gracefully or melts under load.

title: Database Indexing & Query Optimization
description: A staff-level deep dive into how relational indexes physically work, how the cost-based optimizer decides between sequential, index, and bitmap scans, and how to design composite, covering, partial, and expression indexes that survive write amplification. Covers reading EXPLAIN ANALYZE, sargability, the N+1 problem, and replacing OFFSET pagination with keyset seeks.

title: Databases & SQL
description: A staff-level deep dive into relational modeling, indexing, query planning, transactions and isolation, MVCC, and scaling SQL databases under real production load.

title: Domain-Driven Design
description: A pragmatic, staff-level guide to Domain-Driven Design — from ubiquitous language and bounded contexts down to aggregates, invariants, and the transactional consistency boundaries that make or break a model.

title: Design Patterns (GoF)
description: A staff-level tour of the 15 most interview-relevant Gang of Four patterns, with the problem each solves, when to reach for it, when to avoid it, and idiomatic Java.

title: Distributed Transactions & Saga
description: A staff-level treatment of why ACID across service boundaries is impractical, how 2PC trades availability for consistency, and how Sagas, the transactional Outbox, and TCC achieve correctness under eventual consistency. Covers compensations, idempotency, and the isolation anomalies that bite teams in production.

title: Docker
description: A staff-level deep dive into Docker images, the layered union filesystem, Dockerfile build mechanics, and the production techniques that turn a bloated 1.2GB image into a lean, secure, fast-building artifact.

title: Event Sourcing & CQRS
description: A staff-level treatment of event sourcing (persisting state as an append-only log of immutable domain events) and CQRS (splitting the write and read models), how they combine with DDD aggregates, and the operational realities of eventual consistency, projections, versioning, and idempotency. The central theme: these are two independent patterns whose power and cost must each be justified, not adopted reflexively.

title: Git
description: A deep, mental-model-first tour of Git's object graph, history-rewriting commands, recovery techniques, and the branching strategies senior engineers are expected to reason about under pressure.

title: GraphQL
description: A deep, production-grade tour of GraphQL — schema design, resolvers, the N+1 problem and DataLoader, federation, pagination, and why caching and rate limiting get genuinely harder than with REST.

title: gRPC & Protobuf
description: A staff-level deep dive into gRPC and Protocol Buffers: schema design, HTTP/2 multiplexing, the four RPC types, deadlines, interceptors, and the wire-compatibility rules that keep distributed systems from breaking.

title: Helm
description: Master Helm as a production-grade Kubernetes package manager: templated charts, value overrides, releases and rollbacks, dependencies, hooks, and the subtle Go-templating gotchas that bite teams in real incidents.

title: Hexagonal Architecture
description: Master Ports & Adapters: isolate your domain from frameworks, databases, and transports so business logic stays testable, replaceable, and durable.

title: Java Concurrency
description: A staff-level tour of Java concurrency from the Java Memory Model and happens-before to atomics, executors, CompletableFuture, and Project Loom virtual threads.

title: JavaScript
description: A staff-level deep dive into JavaScript's runtime model, async semantics, closures, prototypes, and the subtle footguns that separate senior engineers from the rest.

title: Apache Kafka
description: A staff-level deep dive into Apache Kafka's distributed commit log: partitioning and ordering guarantees, replication and ISR, delivery semantics, idempotent producers and transactions, and how to build consumers that survive rebalances, retries, and poison pills.

title: Kubernetes
description: A staff-level deep dive into how Kubernetes schedules, runs, and self-heals containerized workloads — from etcd and the control plane to probes, resource limits, and the failure modes that page you at 3am.

title: Kustomize
description: Master template-free Kubernetes configuration management with Kustomize's base-plus-overlay model, strategic and JSON6902 patches, and generator hashing.

title: Load Balancing
description: A staff-level treatment of load balancing as the backbone of horizontal scale and availability: Layer 4 vs Layer 7 trade-offs, balancing algorithms from round robin to consistent hashing, health checking, TLS termination, global routing, and the operational realities of retry storms and connection draining during deploys.

title: Message Queues & Async Messaging
description: A staff-level treatment of asynchronous messaging with brokers: queue versus publish/subscribe versus log semantics, RabbitMQ's exchange/binding model, cloud queues like SQS, and the delivery-guarantee realities (at-least-once plus idempotency, the exactly-once myth, DLQs, ordering, and backpressure) that separate a robust system from a fragile one.

title: Microservices
description: A staff-level deep dive into designing, communicating between, and operating microservices without accidentally building a distributed monolith.

title: Networking & HTTP
description: A staff-engineer tour of the network stack beneath web applications: TCP/IP versus OSI, the TCP handshake and congestion control, DNS and TLS, the evolution of HTTP/1.1 to HTTP/2 to HTTP/3 over QUIC, and the semantics of methods, caching, cookies, and CORS. The emphasis is on the trade-offs and failure modes that show up in production latency budgets and debugging sessions.

title: OAuth 2.0, OIDC & JWT
description: A staff-level treatment of delegated authorization (OAuth 2.0), federated authentication (OIDC), and the JWT token format that ties them together, with an emphasis on grant selection, rigorous token validation, and the vulnerability classes that defeat naive implementations.

title: Observability
description: A staff-level deep dive into the three pillars of observability, SLOs and error budgets, Prometheus and PromQL, distributed tracing, and the failure modes that cripple monitoring systems in production.

title: Optimistic Locking
description: Optimistic locking lets concurrent writers proceed without holding row locks, detecting conflicts at commit time via a version token so only the first writer wins and the rest retry. This guide covers the lost-update problem, version columns, JPA @Version, CAS and ABA, conditional SQL, HTTP ETags, DynamoDB conditional writes, and how it relates to isolation levels and MVCC.

title: Quarkus
description: A Kubernetes-native Java framework that moves work from runtime to build time, producing tiny, fast-starting GraalVM native binaries ideal for serverless and high-density containers.

title: REST APIs
description: A staff-level deep dive into designing REST APIs that survive scale, partial failure, and a decade of clients — covering constraints, idempotency, pagination, versioning, caching, and security.

title: Application Security: AuthN & AuthZ
description: A staff-level walkthrough of authentication, authorization, tokens, OAuth2/OIDC, and the real-world tradeoffs that decide whether your auth layer holds up under attack.

title: SOLID Principles
description: A staff-level deep dive into the five SOLID principles with bad-to-good Java refactors, the design forces behind each one, and the trade-offs that matter in production systems.

title: Spring Boot
description: Master Spring Boot from IoC and auto-configuration to the AOP-proxy gotchas around @Transactional that bite senior engineers in production.

title: SQL vs NoSQL
description: A staff-level comparison of the relational model and the four NoSQL families, covering ACID vs BASE, access-pattern-first modeling, consistency and CAP positioning of real engines, and how to actually choose a data store rather than chase a label.

title: System Design
description: A staff-level walkthrough of the canonical system design interview problems — URL shortener, chat, payments, food delivery, and notifications — with requirements, architecture, data models, scaling, caching, and failure handling for each.

title: Testing & TDD
description: A staff-level tour of how to design, structure, and reason about automated tests so they catch real bugs, stay fast, and survive refactors.

title: TypeScript
description: A staff-level tour of TypeScript's structural type system, from generics and conditional/mapped types to the footguns of any, as, and declaration merging that bite teams in production.

title: Web Security (OWASP)
description: A staff-engineer's guide to the OWASP Top 10 — understanding the real attack mechanics behind injection, broken access control, SSRF, and cryptographic failures, and the defenses that actually hold up in production.
